skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini native image models through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Run Gemini image generation and image editing workflows through inference.sh.
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-command examples and several hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md. Review confirms the skill intentionally invokes the inference.sh CLI and remote Gemini image services, while the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives on model names and table text. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run infsh commands for login, app discovery, sample generation, and Gemini image generation. This is a real external command surface and should be reviewed for user-provided prompt quoting and local CLI trust, but the commands align with the stated skill purpose.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines contain concrete infsh command examples. The behavior is intentional for this CLI-based image skill, so the risk is real but not evidence of malware.

medium_findings.title: Remote Image Service and Optional Search Grounding
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on inference.sh and Google Gemini image apps, and it documents optional Google Search grounding. Prompts and image URLs may be sent to external services, so users should avoid sensitive inputs unless they accept that data flow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded inference.sh documentation links, a remote image URL example, and enable_google_search are present. These show intended network behavior rather than hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at lines containing model descriptions, example prompts, or table text. No cryptographic operation, hash selection, or security-sensitive algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not implement or configure cryptography. They are ordinary descriptive content, so confidence in an actual cryptographic weakness is very low.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The skill includes links to inference.sh documentation, a hosted preview image, and an example input image URL. These are visible references, not hidden callbacks, but they confirm external network dependencies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are clearly visible and support the documented workflow. They are low severity because no secret collection or covert transfer is shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access for infsh Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access scoped to infsh commands. This allows local command execution through the inference.sh CLI and requires users to trust the installed CLI and its configured account.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly show Bash-mediated CLI use. The command scope is narrow, which keeps this at medium risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Data Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: Prompts and image references are sent to third-party inference services when the documented commands run. This can expose creative briefs, source images, or user-provided URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples show prompts, images, and search grounding being passed to remote model apps. This is expected behavior, but it is still a privacy-relevant pattern.

content.value_statement: Image generation workflows often need model-specific CLI commands and input fields. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear instructions for Gemini image generation through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Gemini text-to-image examples through the infsh CLI.
Shows how to edit images by passing input image URLs.
Documents Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image app IDs.
Lists supported inputs such as prompts, aspect ratio, resolution, and image count.
Provides commands for app search, sample input generation, and related image skills.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Sends prompts and image references to external model services.
Does not implement image generation itself; it only guides CLI usage.
Model availability and parameters can change outside the skill.

use_cases.title: Concept Art Drafting
use_cases.description: Generate visual directions from short creative briefs before refining a final prompt.

use_cases.title: Product Mockup Exploration
use_cases.description: Create several image options with different aspect ratios, styles, or resolutions.

use_cases.title: Image Editing Experiments
use_cases.description: Modify a supplied image with a direct edit request through Gemini image models.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to create one image of a modern desk setup in natural light.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to generate four logo concepts for a neighborhood bakery. Keep the style simple and friendly.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to edit this image URL. Add soft evening light and keep the original composition.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a High Resolution Campaign Visual
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana with Gemini 3 Pro Image to create a 16:9 4K campaign image for a sustainable travel service.

output_examples.input: Create a photorealistic banana floating above a moonlit ocean.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an inference.sh Gemini image request and returns the generated image result or download reference.

output_examples.input: Make four square coffee shop logo options with a minimalist style.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects the faster Gemini image model and requests four image variations with a square composition.

output_examples.input: Edit this landscape image so the sky has a rainbow.
output_examples.output: The assistant sends the source image reference and edit instruction to Gemini 2.5 Flash Image through inference.sh.

best_practices: Use clear prompts that specify subject, style, composition, lighting, and output size.
Avoid private images or confidential briefs unless external processing is approved.
Choose Gemini 3 Pro Image for quality and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image for speed.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, credentials, or unreleased product details into prompts.
Do not assume every documented parameter works for every Gemini image model.
Do not enable search grounding when a request should stay independent of live web data.

faq.question: What does this skill require?
faq.answer: It requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account before image commands can run.

faq.question: Which models does it document?
faq.answer: It documents Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image app IDs.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill shows how to pass input image references with an edit prompt.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: Yes. Prompts and image references are sent through inference.sh to remote model apps.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential work?
faq.answer: Use caution. Confidential prompts and images should only be used when external processing is approved.

faq.question: Can it browse current information?
faq.answer: The skill documents an optional Google Search grounding flag for supported Gemini image workflows.
